steps.title: Recognize what 'compare' requires
steps.content: The question asks to “compare the causes,” which signals that the answer must present both mechanisms—how Jacobs explains crown shyness and how Ng explains it.

steps.title: Find the answer that juxtaposes both mechanisms
steps.content: Choice C directly contrasts both causes: Jacobs's mechanism is branches brushing, Ng's is shade detection. The other choices fail to present this comparison—A and B name the theories without stating their causes, D explains only Jacobs. That matches choice C — C is correct.
